codeReview.summary: The feature implements init identity/bootstrap behavior broadly as specified (fresh init, rerun handling, SQLCipher preflight, gating, and concurrency lock), but the atomic-failure guarantee has a blocking gap in real failure paths.

issues.description: Artifacts are added to cleanup tracking only after each creation call returns (e.g., `persistIdentity` at line 103 with `createdArtifacts.push` at line 104; same pattern for `persistKey`/`openEncryptedDb` at lines 114-120). If a write/open fails mid-step, partial files can be left behind and will not be removed by `cleanupArtifacts`, which violates the feature's atomic failure handling intent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature verification text still uses Jest-style `--testPathPattern`, but this repository runs Vitest; this causes avoidable verification detours.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json` verificationSteps for this feature includes `npm run test -- --testPathPattern init`; transcript skeleton shows that command failed and was replaced with `npx vitest run test/init.test.ts`; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges also calls this out.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript sequence does not match strict red-first TDD in `mors-worker` skill guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows `src/identity.ts`, `src/init.ts`, and `src/cli.ts` were created before `test/init.test.ts`; `.factory/skills/mors-worker/SKILL.md` Work Procedure step 2 requires adding/extending failing tests first.

summary: Reviewed mission artifacts, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit diff for fd64e8c. Implementation is close to requirements, but atomic cleanup is incomplete for pre-registration failure paths, so this feature review is marked fail pending that fix.
